metadata.title: Tokyo Neighborhood Explorer - Find Your Perfect Area
metadata.description: Interactive map of Tokyo neighborhoods rated by food, nightlife, transport, rent, safety, parks, and more. Find the best area to live in Greater Tokyo.

ratingTooltips.food: Variety and quality of restaurants, cafes, street food, and specialty dining within 10-15 min walk
ratingTooltips.nightlife: Bars, izakaya, clubs, live music venues, and late-night entertainment options
ratingTooltips.transport: Number of train lines, frequency, connections to major hubs, and overall commute convenience
ratingTooltips.rent: Affordability based on actual rent data (1K-1LDK). 10 = cheapest (~¥70k/mo), 1 = most expensive (~¥300k+)
ratingTooltips.safety: Overall neighborhood safety: crime rates, street lighting, family-friendliness, late-night comfort
ratingTooltips.green: Parks, gardens, riverside walks, green spaces, and nature within 15 min walk
ratingTooltips.gym_sports: Fitness centers, gyms, sports facilities, running paths, and athletic amenities nearby
ratingTooltips.vibe: Overall character and charm: cultural identity, street life, local community, architectural interest
ratingTooltips.crowd: Quietness level (inverted: 10 = very peaceful and uncrowded, 1 = extremely busy and packed)
ratingTooltips.daily_essentials: Supermarkets, pharmacies, clinics, banks, laundry, dentists, and other daily necessities within walking distance

editorial.label: Curated
editorial.description: Set by human researcher, may differ from data

filter.weightsDescription: Adjust what matters most to you
filter.noMatch: No stations match — try relaxing filters

station.title: Know this area?
station.description: Share what it's like living near {name} — your tips help others choose.
station.placeholder: What's the vibe? Best spots? Anything to watch out for?

general.title: Got feedback?
general.description: Tell us how we can make this site more useful — missing features, wrong data, new ideas.
general.placeholder: What would make this site better for you?

feedback.rateLimitError: Please wait a few seconds before sending again.
feedback.genericError: Could not send. Please try again.

station.metaDescription: {name} station area: ratings, rent prices, transit times. {description}
station.metaDescriptionFallback: Neighborhood guide for {name} station area in Tokyo.
station.ratingsCaption: Bar fill compares this station to the Tokyo median for that row — hover the bar for pigment detail, the category name for what it measures.
station.ratingsCaptionWithConfidence: Where a colored dot appears, it shows how firm that score is (hover for sources); hues match the key below.
station.barTooltipDev: Tokyo norm is {median} — this station is {dev} {direction} norm.
station.barTooltipDevExact: Tokyo norm is {median} — this station is exactly average.
station.descriptionInProgress: AI research for this station is in progress.
station.seismicProbability: Probability of JMA intensity ≥6.0 in 30 years
station.sourceSeismic: Source: J-SHIS 2024 (National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience)

elevationTiers.very_low: Very low — check flood hazard maps

elevationTooltips.belowSea: Below sea level — this area is in a known flood-prone zone. The Koto/Edogawa lowlands and Arakawa floodplain are at highest risk during typhoons and storm surges.
elevationTooltips.veryLow: Very low elevation — near or below sea level. These areas are susceptible to flooding during heavy rainfall, typhoons, and storm surges. Check Tokyo/prefecture flood hazard maps.
elevationTooltips.low: Low-lying area. Some flood risk during extreme weather events. Generally manageable with proper preparation.
elevationTooltips.moderate: Moderate to high elevation. Lower flood risk from river/coastal flooding.

seismicTooltips.veryHigh: This area has significantly higher risk, often due to soft alluvial soil or reclaimed land that amplifies seismic waves.
seismicTooltips.high: This is a relatively high-risk area. Building earthquake resistance and emergency preparedness are important considerations.
seismicTooltips.moderate: Moderate risk. Standard earthquake preparedness measures are recommended.
seismicTooltips.low: Lower risk area, typically on firmer bedrock. Standard precautions still apply.
